title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How do you find the midsegment of a trapezoid?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear explanation of how to calculate or identify the midsegment of a trapezoid. The midsegment is defined as the segment connecting the midpoints of the non-parallel sides (legs) of a trapezoid and is equal to the average of the lengths of the two bases. Relevant documents should provide this definition and formula, while irrelevant documents may discuss unrelated topics such as area calculations, symmetry, or midlines in triangles.
